channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Punk rock pioneer battling stage 4 cancer and brain tumor
item.description: His family is holding a fundraiser to help pay for a nurse, physical therapy, and "day-to-day vital living expenses.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams vowed to regain the public’s trust Tuesday as the Justice Department moved to halt his criminal corruption case, an extraordinary directive that officials said would free him up to assist in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: President Donald Trump’s most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, has made a rare public appearance at the White House to defend the swift and extensive cuts he’s pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there may be mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Punk rock pioneer battling stage 4 cancer and brain tumor; family asks for help
item.description: His family is holding a fundraiser to help pay for a nurse, physical therapy, and "day-to-day vital living expenses.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chinese spy balloon equipped with tech from at least 5 US firms: report
item.description: Chinese spy balloon that floated across the U.S. before being shot down off the South Carolina coast in 2023 was carrying technology from at least five American companies, report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Florida 'brothel' allegedly run by illegal immigrants shut down after victim's desperate phone call
item.description: Florida police have arrested two Guatemalan illegal immigrants for allegedly trafficking two women being held against their will at a residence in Lake Worth Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states, including Illinois
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Murphy looks to be guiding force for Democrats in new Trump era
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) is taking a front-and-center role in the efforts to push Democrats toward becoming the party of economic populism and to challenge President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from long-standing norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illega
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New Jersey driver awarded $13M after state troopers mistakenly believed her stroke was sign of intoxication
item.description: A New Jersey driver was awarded nearly $13 million after she was arrested by a trooper who mistakenly believed she was drunk when she had a stroke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue city police sergeants say they're paid less than subordinates as billions go to migrants
item.description: NYPD sergeants are seeking a new contract with New York City as many make less than the rank-and-file officers they supervise, union president says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombian nationals arrested in connection with dozens of home burglaries in Oregon: police
item.description: The Tigard Police Department have arrested four Colombian nationals in connection with a string of 39 home burglary cases allegedly targeting Asian-American households since September.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Venezuela sends 2 planes to US to return migrants, signaling a potential improvement in relations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Two Venezuelan planes flew to the United States on Monday and returned home with deported Venezuelans, signaling a possible improvement in relations between longtime diplomatic adversaries and a victory for President Donald Trump in his efforts to get more countries to take the
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FEMA says it's halting payments for migrant housing in New York after Musk blasts money for hotels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting head of the federal agency responsible for responding to disasters said Monday that he’s suspending payments sent to New York City to house migrants and that staff who made them will be held accountable, after Elon Musk blasted the transactions on his social media platfo
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at 3 schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the 24-year-old brother of the Parkland school shooter, was arrested Feb. 4 for trespassing at multiple school properties, including Wilson Middle School, Wilson High School, and the Valley Career and Technical Center.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge directs Trump administration to comply with order to unfreeze federal grants
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island ordered the Trump administration to comply with his order to unfreeze federal grants, after attorneys general for several Democratic states claimed the directive was not being fulfilled. In a short order Monday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5
